slides.title: Market Size Timeline
slides.goal: Show how the grounded evidence changes over time.
key_points: The mid-market analytics TAM reached $3.4B in 2024 with 28% projected CAGR through 2027.

Market Size Timeline The North American mid-market analytics segment grew from $2.1B in 2022 to $3.4B in 2024, with projected CAGR of 28% through 2027. Self-serve analytics adoption doubled year-over-year in the 100-500 employee segment. The mid-market analytics TAM reached $3.4B in 2024 with 28% projected CAGR through 2027.

slides.title: Competitive Landscape
slides.goal: Contrast grounded market options without losing source traceability.
key_points: Mid-market buyers prioritize time-to-value over feature depth, creating an opening for platforms with sub-week onboarding.

Competitive Landscape Incumbent tools like Tableau and Looker dominate enterprise but leave mid-market underserved on pricing and onboarding speed. New entrants compete on time-to-value: average onboarding dropped from 6 weeks to 3 days for modern platforms. Mid-market buyers prioritize time-to-value over feature depth, creating an opening for platforms with sub-week onboarding.
slides.speaker_notes: Ground this slide in src-01:sec-02 and keep the structure editable.

slides.title: Launch Sequence
slides.goal: Turn grounded evidence into an ordered action path.
key_points: A three-phase launch sequence from beta to PLG to channel partners de-risks market entry while building social proof.

Launch Sequence Phase 1: invite-only beta with 50 design partners (July). Phase 2: public launch with self-serve signup and PLG motion (September). Phase 3: channel partner enablement and outbound sales overlay (November). A three-phase launch sequence from beta to PLG to channel partners de-risks market entry while building social proof.

step_labels: public launch with self-serve signup and PLG motion (September)
channel partner enablement and outbound sales overlay (November)

slides.speaker_notes: Ground this slide in src-02:sec-01 and keep the structure editable.

slides.title: Pricing and Margins
slides.goal: Contrast grounded market options without losing source traceability.
key_points: An $18K ACV with 82% gross margin and sub-12-month CAC payback creates a sustainable mid-market unit economics profile.

Pricing and Margins Target ACV of $18K positions AnalyticsPro below enterprise incumbents ($50K+) but above freemium tools ($0-5K). Gross margin at 82% with CAC payback under 12 months at projected conversion rates of 8% from free trial. An $18K ACV with 82% gross margin and sub-12-month CAC payback creates a sustainable mid-market unit economics profile.
slides.speaker_notes: Ground this slide in src-03:sec-01 and keep the structure editable.

slides.title: Beta NPS and Feature Requests
slides.goal: Surface the grounded numeric signal that should shape the decision.
key_points: Beta NPS of 62 and 47-minute time-to-first-insight validate the product-market fit hypothesis for mid-market self-serve analytics.

Beta NPS and Feature Requests Beta cohort NPS averaged 62 across 38 respondents. Top feature requests: Slack integration (71%), scheduled reports (64%), and custom dashboards (58%). Median time-to-first-insight was 47 minutes, beating the 2-hour target by 60%. Beta NPS of 62 and 47-minute time-to-first-insight validate the product-market fit hypothesis for mid-market self-serve analytics.
slides.speaker_notes: Ground this slide in src-04:sec-01 and keep the structure editable.
